calendar.matters:
Petition For Instructions Regarding Construction Of The Montero Trust And Disposition Of Trust Assets
Off Calendar Feb-10-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jon R. Vaught for Beneficiary, Jacqueline Aivars, via Court Call.
David Packard for Lindsay Montero in the Estate of Joe Montero Jr.
Yulissa Zulaica for Petitioner, Jocasta Montero Aleman.

The Court announces its tentative ruling regarding proper venue in San Mateo. Mr. Packard stated that since the petition has been filed, parties have gone through mediation, have agreed to 90-95% of the terms regarding distribution issues, are apart on costs, and request 30 days continuance. Ms. Zulaica stated the main reason the petition was filed in San Francisco was because there is another property in San Francisco, that parties are close with 1 or 2 lingering issues, she has no concern with a continuance, and ask for a dismissal if not settled to have partition action in San Mateo. Ms. Zulaica further stated that 30 days is enough time to see if parties settle. Mr. Vaught stated parties are close to a final agreement and agree with a short continuance. The Court noted that any settlement would need to be confirmed or approved by San Mateo not San Francisco. Mr. Packard stated the settlement will be part of the San Mateo administration. The Court stated dismissing now or in 30 days makes no difference. Ms. Zulaica requested matter be dismissed without prejudice so she can re-file in San Mateo.

The Petition for Instructions Regarding Construction of the Montero Trust and Disposition of Trust Assets is taken under submission to be dismissed without prejudice for venue purposes. Any follow up needs to happen in San Mateo.

Judge: Joeseph M. Quinn. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Matter not reported.

Petition For Instructions Regarding Construction Of The Montero Trust And Disposition Of Trust Assets
Off Calendar Jan-22-2019 Continued To Apr-23-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Yulissa Zulaica for Jocasta Aleman (Petitioner).
David Packard for Lindsey Montero (Administrator of the Estate of Joseph, Jr.)
Gregory Aker for Jacqueline Ivars (Beneficiary).

Ms. Zulaica addressed the issue of venue stating that the Petitioner is an Heir, is a Trustee and lives in Spain, that there are three San Francisco properties and that this is the principal place of administration. Ms. Zulaica also stated that the Attorney, the bank accounts and the real estate are in San Francisco, made reference to Probate Codes 17005 and 17005(c) and stated that the day to day tasks take place here because her Client is in Spain. Mr. Aker stated that both sides agree that venue is in San Francisco. Mr. Packard informed the Court that they have agreed to mediation and to completing it within 90-days. The Petition for Instructions Regarding Construction of Trust, etc. is continued to April 23, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If the matter is not resolved, Ms. Zulaica is to provide any additional information with regard to venue no later than April 16, 2019.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Petition For Instructions Regarding Construction Of The Montero Trust And Disposition Of Trust Assets
Off Calendar Jan-08-2019 Continued To Jan-22-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
